name: Chiggi Stats
description: Detailed playback statistics and reporting for Jellyfin. Tracks what every user is watching, how long they watch, and provides a filterable dashboard accessible directly inside Jellyfin. Supports filtering by user, date range, and media type. Data is stored in a local SQLite database with configurable retention.
overview: Detailed playback statistics with user/date/media filters.

versions.changelog: Fix: GetAuthenticatedUserId() now checks 'Jellyfin-UserId' claim first (Jellyfin 10.11 InternalClaimTypes.UserId) — wrong claim name was causing all data endpoints to return 401 and show 'Failed to load data.'. Fix: remove is="emby-button" from tab buttons so page CSS fully controls their appearance — emby-button component CSS was overriding inactive tab button background to grey regardless of !important.

Fix: replace raw fetch() with ApiClient.ajax() so Jellyfin's full MediaBrowser auth header is sent — token-only header was rejected causing all API calls to fail. Fix: add !important to active tab button CSS to override emby-button component styles that were making all tabs appear grey.

Fix: delete entire runtimes/ directory from plugin zip after flattening native libs. Jellyfin's plugin scanner crawls ALL subdirectories recursively for *.dll — any native DLL anywhere in the tree disables the plugin. runtimes/win-*/native/e_sqlite3.dll was causing BadImageFormatException even when not at the root.

Fix: exclude Windows native e_sqlite3.dll from plugin root — Jellyfin's plugin scanner tried to load it as a managed assembly causing BadImageFormatException on startup. Only Linux/macOS native libs are now flattened to the plugin directory.

Feat: full reporting dashboard with Overview, Playback, Movies, Series, Seasons, Episodes, Music, Box Sets, Users, and Devices tabs. Admin-only inventory reports backed by live Jellyfin library data. Paginated playback history with date/user/media-type filters. Pure CSS bar chart — no external CDN dependencies.
Fix: SQLite native library now found correctly in Jellyfin plugin context (no more Malfunctioned). Fix: database initialisation split into separate statements to prevent startup crash.
Initial release. Playback tracking with SQLite storage, activity log integration, and in-Jellyfin stats dashboard.

name: Trakt
description: Sync your Jellyfin library to trakt.tv and scrobble your watch status. This is a fixed fork of the official Jellyfin Trakt plugin addressing 409 Conflict errors, rate-limit handling, duplicate watch history entries, TV show scrobbling failures in Jellyfin 10.11, and more.
overview: Sync library and scrobble to trakt.tv — fixed fork with bug fixes.

versions.changelog: Fix: add ExcludeAssets=runtime to Jellyfin package references — without it, Jellyfin's own assemblies (MediaBrowser.Controller, MediaBrowser.Model, Jellyfin.Data, etc.) and all transitive deps (EFCore, Polly, ICU4N, etc.) were bundled into the plugin zip. Jellyfin's PluginLoadContext loaded duplicate instances of these assemblies, causing IHasWebPages type identity check to fail silently — plugin loaded but settings page never appeared.
Fix: restored Trakt settings/configuration page button (missing RootNamespace caused embedded resource path mismatch).
Fork of official v27 with fixes: #265 rate-limit HasFlag bug, #266 409 Conflict crash, #226 duplicate history (HashSet dedup), #258 null Series on TV scrobble.
